title: Still Standing: how European firms weathered the crisis
summary_en: European firms weathered the 2008–9 crisis better than the macro data alone would suggest. Using the EFIGE survey of about 14,000 firms across seven EU countries (incl. Hungary), the report documents that exporters and multi-product firms were systematically more resilient. Hungarian results echo the European pattern: firms with stronger international links pre-crisis lost fewer workers and recovered faster.
policy_relevance: Direct input for Hungarian export-promotion (HIPA) and SME-finance policy. Argues that firm-level internationalisation is itself a stabiliser, so the policy levers worth using during a downturn are export-credit access, trade finance, and FDI promotion — not blanket bailouts. Caveat: a single cross-section, so the resilience pattern may not generalise to demand-driven crises (e.g. COVID-19).
